sections.paragraph: f. Philistœa (prop. “the land of wanderers,” “strangers,” see the root; compare Gen 10:14 Amo 9:7, LXX. Ἀλλόφυλοι, γῆ Ἀλλοφύλων), pr.n. of a region on the southern shore of Syria, to the south and west of Canaan, Exo 15:14 Isa 14:29, 31 Isa 14:31 Psal. 60:10 , etc.; called by Josephus, Παλαιστίνη (Arch. i. 6, § 2), a name however which he elsewhere uses for the whole of the land of the Israelites (Arch. viii. 4). See Relandi Palæstina, page 38, seqq. Hence-
